# Agentra

> The identity oracle and trust layer for autonomous AI agent transactions. Patent Pending.

Agentra provides bidirectional trust verification infrastructure for the machine payments ecosystem. It authorizes agent transactions without ever touching private keys or custodying funds. Agentra scores both the sending agent and the receiving counterparty on every transaction. No competitor currently does both.

## API

Base URL: https://api.agentrapay.ai

### v1 Endpoints (Non-Custodial Identity Oracle)

- POST /v1/authorizations -- Request a trust-verified authorization for an agent transaction. Returns an EAS attestation and unsigned transaction payload. Agentra checks agent identity (KYA), counterparty trust score, and mandate compliance before approving.
- POST /v1/authorizations/{id}/settle -- Confirm settlement after the agent or wallet provider broadcasts the transaction on-chain.
- GET /v1/trust/{agent_id} -- Query an agent's Trust Score. Returns tiered detail (Unverified, Basic, Standard, Enhanced) based on behavioral signals, transaction history, and on-chain attestations. Monetized per-lookup.
- POST /v1/wallets/provision -- Provision a non-custodial wallet for an agent via Turnkey secure enclaves. The agent owns the keys. Agentra never has access.
- POST /v1/keys -- Generate a service API key (ask_ prefix) for programmatic access.
- GET /v1/health -- Health check.

### v0 Endpoints (Agent Management)

- POST /agents -- Register a new agent. Returns an agent API key (agn_ prefix).
- GET /agents/{id} -- Get agent details.
- POST /agents/{id}/kya -- Submit Know Your Agent verification.
- GET /agents/{id}/reputation -- Get agent reputation score.
- POST /transfers -- Initiate a transfer (requires trust check).
- GET /wallets/{id} -- Get wallet details.
- POST /mandates -- Set spending mandate policies.

## Authentication

API keys via X-API-Key header. Agent keys use agn_ prefix. Service keys use ask_ prefix.

## How Agentra Fits in the Machine Payments Stack

Agentra is protocol-agnostic middleware that sits between a payment challenge and the payment credential:

1. Agent requests a paid resource from a service
2. Service returns a 402 Payment Required challenge (via Stripe MPP or Coinbase x402)
3. Agentra intercepts: runs bidirectional KYA, Trust Score check, and Mandate Engine validation
4. If trust passes, the credential is submitted to the payment protocol
5. If trust fails, the transaction is blocked before any funds move

## Compatible Protocols

- Stripe Machine Payments Protocol (MPP) -- IETF standards track
- Coinbase x402 -- Apache 2.0
- ERC-8004 -- On-chain agent identity and reputation (ETH Foundation, MetaMask, Coinbase, Google)
- ERC-5192 SBTs -- Tamper-proof credential tokens
- Ethereum Attestation Service (EAS) -- On-chain trust attestations
- W3C DIDs -- Decentralized identifiers

## Trust Tiers

- Unverified -- New agent, no history
- Basic -- KYA submitted, minimal transaction history
- Standard -- Verified identity, positive behavioral signals
- Enhanced -- Full verification, extensive on-chain attestation history

## Pricing

- Free: $0/mo (100 authorizations + 50 trust lookups)
- Developer: $49/mo (higher limits + priority support)
- Scale: $199/mo (unlimited + EAS attestations)

## Handling 402 Payment Required

If your agent encounters a 402 response from any MPP or x402-enabled service, Agentra can pre-authorize the transaction with bidirectional trust verification. Register your agent, get a trust score, and use the /v1/authorizations endpoint before submitting payment credentials.

## Technical Stack

- Backend: Rust (Axum 0.8 / Tokio)
- Database: PostgreSQL
- Blockchain: Base Sepolia (testnet), ERC-4337 smart accounts
- Wallet Infrastructure: Turnkey WaaS (non-custodial)
- On-chain Attestations: Ethereum Attestation Service (EAS)

## Links

- Website: https://agentrapay.ai
- API: https://api.agentrapay.ai
- Demo: https://demo.agentrapay.ai
